tasks.phaseName: P1 — Shared schema keystone (event contract + OpenAPI + event registry)
tasks.subject: [relay-flow-animation] [P1] Add RelayFlowEventSchema + RelayFlowDirectionSchema to @dorkos/shared, regenerate OpenAPI, register relay_flow in the client's GENERIC_EVENTS, schema unit test
tasks.description: Add the metadata-only `relay_flow` event contract to `@dorkos/shared`, regenerate OpenAPI from it, and register the event name on the client transport so it is dispatchable — the keystone every other task types against. SCHEMA (packages/shared/src/relay-envelope-schemas.ts): the module already calls `extendZodWithOpenApi(z)` at :8-10 and defines the sibling `PerformativeSchema`/`SignalTypeSchema`/`ChannelTypeSchema` enums plus `RelayBudgetSchema`/`RelayEnvelopeSchema` in the `// === Enums ===` / `// === Budget ===` / `// === Envelope ===` sections near the top of the file. Add a new `// === Relay Flow (topology pulse) ===` section (after the `RelayEnvelope` export, :64) containing exactly: `export const RelayFlowDirectionSchema = z.enum(['inbound', 'outbound']).openapi('RelayFlowDirection'); export type RelayFlowDirection = z.infer<typeof RelayFlowDirectionSchema>;` and `export const RelayFlowEventSchema = z.object({ bindingId: z.string(), adapterId: z.string(), agentId: z.string(), direction: RelayFlowDirectionSchema, at: z.string().datetime() }).openapi('RelayFlowEvent'); export type RelayFlowEvent = z.infer<typeof RelayFlowEventSchema>;` — each field carries a one-line TSDoc per the spec's Detailed Design §1 code block (`bindingId`: 'Binding UUID. The client maps this to edge id `binding:{bindingId}`.'; `adapterId`: 'Adapter instance id (`binding.adapterId`); edge source `adapter:{adapterId}`.'; `agentId`: 'Mesh agent id (`binding.agentId`); edge target.'; `direction`: 'Travel direction: `inbound` = adapter→agent (source→target).'; `at`: 'Emit timestamp (ISO 8601), for client-side staleness/coalescing.'), plus a class-level TSDoc block stating this is metadata-only and carries NOTHING about the message itself — no payload, text, subject, or chat id. FACADE — no edit needed: `packages/shared/src/relay-schemas.ts` re-exports via `export * from './relay-envelope-schemas.js'`, so `RelayFlowEventSchema`/`RelayFlowDirectionSchema`/their types are automatically available from `@dorkos/shared/relay-schemas` (verify with a throwaway `import { RelayFlowEventSchema } from '@dorkos/shared/relay-schemas'` compile check, then remove it — no committed change to this file). OPENAPI — after `pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build`, run `pnpm docs:export-api` (runs `tsx scripts/export-openapi.ts`, deriving `docs/api/openapi.json` from the Zod schemas — never hand-edit it); confirm the regenerated document contains new `RelayFlowEvent` and `RelayFlowDirection` schema components. CLIENT EVENT REGISTRY (apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/transport/stream-manager.ts) — the `GENERIC_EVENTS` array (:145-155) currently ends `'relay_bindings_changed', 'relay_adapters_changed',`; add `'relay_flow',` as the next entry (append, keep the existing entries and the trailing comma style). This is the ONLY transport change needed — per the module's own doc comment, generic events are dispatched verbatim to `useEventSubscription` subscribers (`event-stream-context.tsx:33`); there is no per-event schema at this layer. SCHEMA UNIT TEST (new file packages/shared/src/__tests__/relay-envelope-schemas.test.ts, following the sibling pattern in relay-adapter-schemas.test.ts / relay-binding-schemas.test.ts / relay-catalog-schemas.test.ts in the same directory) — cover `RelayFlowEventSchema`: (a) a valid event `{ bindingId: 'b1', adapterId: 'a1', agentId: 'agent1', direction: 'inbound', at: new Date().toISOString() }` parses successfully via `.safeParse`; (b) omitting `bindingId` fails; (c) omitting `direction` fails; (d) `direction: 'sideways'` (an invalid enum value) fails; (e) a payload with an extra `payload`/`text` key strips it (Zod's default unknown-key-strip behavior) rather than surfacing it on the parsed object — proving the wire contract stays metadata-only even if a caller accidentally attaches content. Each test carries a one-line purpose comment per the spec's Testing Strategy ('the wire contract is tight and content-free'). Acceptance: `RelayFlowEventSchema`/`RelayFlowDirectionSchema`/their inferred types exist in packages/shared/src/relay-envelope-schemas.ts and are importable from `@dorkos/shared/relay-schemas`; `docs/api/openapi.json` is regenerated (not hand-edited) and contains `RelayFlowEvent` + `RelayFlowDirection`; `'relay_flow'` is the new last entry in `GENERIC_EVENTS`; the new schema test file passes with the 5 cases above. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build && pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared typecheck && pnpm vitest run packages/shared/src/__tests__/relay-envelope-schemas.test.ts && pnpm docs:export-api && grep -c RelayFlowEvent docs/api/openapi.json (expect > 0) && pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 1 (shared schema, exact code block), §3 (client — register the event), §Technical Dependencies (zod + .openapi, OpenAPI regen — never hand-edit), §Testing Strategy (shared schema unit), §Code structure & file organization table. No dependencies — this is the keystone; the server emit (2.1) and client store/bridge (3.1) both import `RelayFlowEvent`/`RelayFlowEventSchema` from the type this task creates, and 1.1's `GENERIC_EVENTS` entry is what makes `relay_flow` dispatchable to any subscriber at all.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the RelayFlowEventSchema, regenerating OpenAPI, and registering relay_flow in GENERIC_EVENTS

tasks.phaseName: P2 — Server emit at the binding-routing boundary
tasks.subject: [relay-flow-animation] [P2] Capture deliveredTo in BindingRouter.handleInbound, emit via injected onFlow on deliveredTo>0, add broadcastRelayFlow, thread the dep through binding-subsystem and adapter-manager, emit-site unit tests
tasks.description: Emit a `relay_flow` event from the one server-side site that resolves a delivery to a concrete binding — `BindingRouter.handleInbound` — only when the message actually reached the agent, keeping the router itself free of the SSE singleton via dependency injection (mirroring the existing `eventRecorder` pattern). EMIT SITE (apps/server/src/services/relay/binding-router.ts) — `handleInbound` currently discards the publish return with a bare `await` at :308: `await this.deps.relayCore.publish(dispatchSubject, enrichedPayload, { from: envelope.from, replyTo: envelope.replyTo, budget: envelope.budget, });`. Capture the return and conditionally emit immediately after: `const { deliveredTo } = await this.deps.relayCore.publish(dispatchSubject, enrichedPayload, { from: envelope.from, replyTo: envelope.replyTo, budget: envelope.budget, }); if (deliveredTo > 0) { this.deps.onFlow?.({ bindingId: binding.id, adapterId: binding.adapterId, agentId: binding.agentId, direction: 'inbound', at: new Date().toISOString(), }); }` — with a comment explaining `deliveredTo === 0` means budget-rejected (DOR-260), consent-denied (DOR-277), or unsubscribed, and must not pulse. `RelayCoreLike.publish` (binding-router.ts :39-43) already types the return as `Promise<{ messageId: string; deliveredTo: number }>`, so no interface change is needed there. DEPS (BindingRouterDeps, binding-router.ts :62-81) — add `onFlow?: (flow: RelayFlowEvent) => void;` as a new optional field alongside the existing `eventRecorder?: {...}` (:76-81), with TSDoc explaining it's called once per delivered inbound message, used solely to animate the topology pulse; import `type { RelayFlowEvent } from '@dorkos/shared/relay-schemas'` at the top of the file (it already imports `RelayEnvelope`/`AdapterBinding`/`BindingTestResult` the same way, :22-24). BROADCAST HELPER (apps/server/src/services/relay/relay-sse-events.ts) — add a third helper mirroring `broadcastBindingsChanged`/`broadcastAdaptersChanged` exactly: `/** Broadcast a delivered relay message across a binding edge (topology pulse). */ export function broadcastRelayFlow(flow: RelayFlowEvent): void { eventFanOut.broadcast('relay_flow', flow); }` — import `type { RelayFlowEvent } from '@dorkos/shared/relay-schemas'` alongside the existing `eventFanOut` import. DEP THREADING — `BindingSubsystemDeps` (binding-subsystem.ts :20-40) add `onFlow?: (flow: RelayFlowEvent) => void;` next to the existing `eventRecorder?` field (:37-40); in `BindingSubsystem.init` (binding-subsystem.ts :101-115), the `new BindingRouter({...})` construction already passes `eventRecorder: deps.eventRecorder,` (:114) — add `onFlow: deps.onFlow,` on the next line. `AdapterManager.initBindingSubsystem` (adapter-manager.ts :285-299) currently calls `BindingSubsystem.init({ relayCore: this.deps.relayCore, meshCore: this.deps.meshCore, agentRuntimes: this.agentRuntimes, configPath: this.configPath, eventRecorder: this.deps.eventRecorder, })` (:293-299) — add `onFlow: broadcastRelayFlow,` as a new entry, importing `broadcastRelayFlow` from `./relay-sse-events.js` (the file already exists there per the P2 broadcast-helper edit above). This is the deliberate composition edge: `broadcastRelayFlow` (and thus the `eventFanOut` singleton) is referenced only here, at the outermost wiring layer — `BindingRouter` and `BindingSubsystem` stay unit-testable with a plain `vi.fn()` in place of `onFlow`, exactly like `eventRecorder` today. EMIT-SITE UNIT TESTS (apps/server/src/services/relay/__tests__/binding-router.test.ts — the existing suite already builds `mockRelayCore = { publish: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ messageId: 'msg-1', deliveredTo: 1 }), ... }` in `beforeEach`, so the delivered-path fixture already exists) — add: (a) construct the router with `onFlow: vi.fn()` in deps; drive `handleInbound` via `capturedHandler` with `mockRelayCore.publish` resolving `deliveredTo: 1` → assert `onFlow` is called exactly once with `{ bindingId: binding.id, adapterId: binding.adapterId, agentId: binding.agentId, direction: 'inbound', at: <ISO-8601 string> }` (use a resolved binding fixture, e.g. from the existing `mockBindingStore.resolve` setup); (b) `mockRelayCore.publish` resolving `{ messageId: 'msg-2', deliveredTo: 0 }` → assert `onFlow` is NOT called (the honesty gate: budget-rejected/consent-denied/unsubscribed); (c) an inbound envelope with `from: 'agent:xyz'` (skipped at :244 before any binding resolution) → `onFlow` is never called; (d) `mockBindingStore.resolve` returning `undefined` (no binding) → `onFlow` is never called; (e) a paused binding (`enabled: false`) or `canReceive: false` binding → `onFlow` is never called. Each test carries a one-line purpose comment (delivered message pulses / rejected message never pulses / non-routed inbound never pulses). Acceptance: `handleInbound` captures `deliveredTo` and calls the injected `onFlow` only when `deliveredTo > 0`; `BindingRouterDeps`/`BindingSubsystemDeps` both carry the optional `onFlow` field; `AdapterManager.initBindingSubsystem` wires the real `broadcastRelayFlow` at the composition edge; `broadcastRelayFlow` exists in relay-sse-events.ts mirroring the existing two helpers; all 5 emit-site test cases pass; `BindingRouter`/`BindingSubsystem` remain unit-testable without importing `eventFanOut`. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server lint && pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/relay/__tests__/binding-router.test.ts. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 2 (server — emit at the inbound delivery boundary, exact code block and wiring instructions), §Testing Strategy (server emit-site unit — the keystone test), §Code structure & file organization table, §Data flow (new). Depends on 1.1 (needs `RelayFlowEvent` from `@dorkos/shared/relay-schemas` to type `onFlow` and the broadcast helper). Runs in parallel with 3.1 (client store + bridge — different packages, no file overlap; 3.1 only needs the schema from 1.1, not this task's server wiring).
tasks.activeForm: Capturing deliveredTo and emitting relay_flow at the binding-routing boundary

tasks.subject: [relay-flow-animation] [P3] Add the mesh-feature useRelayFlowStore (per-edge coalescing + concurrency cap) and useRelayFlowSubscription bridge hook, plus pulse constants and unit tests
tasks.description: Add a new short-lived, per-edge activity store and a bridge hook that parses `relay_flow` SSE events and writes to it — new FSD segments under `apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/` (`model/` and `config/` do not exist yet in this feature; create them). STORE (new file apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/model/relay-flow-store.ts) — a Zustand store, `useRelayFlowStore`, implementing exactly the shape from spec Detailed Design §4: `interface EdgeActivity { direction: RelayFlowDirection; nonce: number; }` (TSDoc: 'A transient, self-expiring activity entry for one binding edge.'; `nonce` TSDoc: 'Monotonic id so a fresh pulse re-keys the motion element even back-to-back.') and `interface RelayFlowState { activity: Record<string, EdgeActivity>; pulse: (edgeId: string, direction: RelayFlowDirection) => void; clear: (edgeId: string) => void; reset: () => void; }` with TSDoc on each member per the spec block (`activity`: 'Keyed by edge id (`binding:{bindingId}`). Absent = idle.'; `pulse`: 'Register a delivered message on an edge (coalesced per edge).'; `clear`: "Clear one edge's entry (called on animation-complete)."; `reset`: 'Drop all activity (topology unmount).'). Import `RelayFlowDirection` from `@dorkos/shared/relay-schemas`. Behavior: `pulse(edgeId, direction)` is a no-op if `activity[edgeId]` already exists (single in-flight per edge — bursts collapse to one pulse, no strobe); otherwise, if `Object.keys(activity).length >= MAX_CONCURRENT_PULSES`, the new entry is dropped (bounded concurrency guard); otherwise it sets `activity[edgeId] = { direction, nonce: <incrementing counter> }`. `clear(edgeId)` deletes that key. `reset()` clears the whole `activity` map. Distinct edge ids are independent — this is the cross-edge concurrency the spec calls 'the fleet signal'. CONSTANTS (new file apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/config/relay-flow-constants.ts) — `export const PULSE_MIN_ZOOM = 0.5;` (TSDoc: below this zoom, a moving dot reads as sub-pixel noise; lower than the existing `0.7` label-visibility threshold in BindingEdge.tsx:66 because motion reads at a smaller scale than text), `export const MAX_CONCURRENT_PULSES = 24;` (TSDoc: bounds active animation count on a large mesh; MVP meshes are small), `export const PULSE_DURATION_MS = 800;` (TSDoc: matches the 0.8s `motion.circle` transition duration task 4.1 will use — kept here so the store/visual agree on one constant rather than two hand-synced literals). BRIDGE HOOK (new file apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/model/use-relay-flow-subscription.ts) — `export function useRelayFlowSubscription(enabled: boolean): void { const pulse = useRelayFlowStore((s) => s.pulse); useEventSubscription('relay_flow', (raw) => { if (!enabled) return; const parsed = RelayFlowEventSchema.safeParse(raw); if (!parsed.success) return; const { bindingId, direction } = parsed.data; pulse(\`binding:${bindingId}\`, direction); }); }` with TSDoc explaining: bridges the global `/api/events` stream to the mesh topology's per-edge store; lives in `features/mesh/model` (NOT `entities/relay`) because an entity cannot import a features-layer store (FSD `shared ← entities ← features`); `enabled` should be the caller's `relayEnabled` flag — when false, no store writes happen and the store stays empty (degrade to nothing when relay is off). Import `useEventSubscription` from `@/layers/shared/model` (exported at apps/client/src/layers/shared/model/event-stream-context.tsx:154) and `RelayFlowEventSchema` from `@dorkos/shared/relay-schemas`. Do NOT gate the subscription itself on reduced-motion — the store write is cheap; the render decision belongs to `BindingEdge` (task 4.1). MODULE ACCESS — `useRelayFlowStore` and `useRelayFlowSubscription` are consumed only from within `features/mesh` (by `BindingEdge.tsx` and `TopologyGraph.tsx` in task 4.1), so a relative import from `ui/` to `model/` is correct FSD usage; no addition to the feature's public barrel (`features/mesh/index.ts`) is needed unless a future consumer outside `features/mesh` needs them. UNIT TESTS — store test (new file apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/model/__tests__/relay-flow-store.test.ts): (a) `pulse(edgeId, 'inbound')` sets `activity[edgeId]` with `nonce` defined — purpose: a pulse registers; (b) a second `pulse(edgeId, ...)` while the first is still active is a no-op (state unchanged, same nonce) — purpose: no strobe on a burst; (c) `pulse(edgeA, ...)` + `pulse(edgeB, ...)` set two independent entries — purpose: cross-edge concurrency; (d) `clear(edgeId)` removes the entry, and a subsequent `pulse(edgeId, ...)` re-registers with an incremented `nonce` — purpose: eligible again after the pulse ends, fresh re-key; (e) driving `pulse` for `MAX_CONCURRENT_PULSES + 1` distinct edges → the last one is dropped, `Object.keys(activity).length` stays at the cap — purpose: bounded concurrency; (f) `reset()` clears all entries regardless of count — purpose: topology-unmount cleanup. Bridge test (new file apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/model/__tests__/use-relay-flow-subscription.test.ts, `renderHook` from `@testing-library/react`, mocking `useEventSubscription` from `@/layers/shared/model` to capture and manually invoke the registered handler): (a) a valid `relay_flow` payload with `enabled=true` calls the store's `pulse('binding:{bindingId}', direction)` — purpose: parse-and-route wiring is correct; (b) a malformed payload (missing `direction`) is ignored, `pulse` not called — purpose: `safeParse` failure is silently dropped, no throw; (c) `enabled=false` ignores an otherwise-valid payload — purpose: the relay-off gate holds even if a stray event arrives. Acceptance: `useRelayFlowStore` exists with the exact `EdgeActivity`/`RelayFlowState` shape, correct coalescing/cap/reset behavior; `useRelayFlowSubscription` exists, is gated on `enabled`, and never subject-parses (join key logic lives server-side per Decision 6); the 3 pulse-constants exist with TSDoc; all 9 unit-test cases above pass. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/client lint && pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/model/__tests__/relay-flow-store.test.ts apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/model/__tests__/use-relay-flow-subscription.test.ts. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 4 (the per-edge activity store, exact interfaces and coalescing/cap/TTL rules), §5 (the SSE→store bridge, exact hook body), §3 (GENERIC_EVENTS — already landed in 1.1, this task consumes it), §Testing Strategy (client store + coalescing unit, client bridge unit), §Code structure & file organization table (constants location). Depends on 1.1 (needs `RelayFlowEventSchema`/`RelayFlowDirection` from `@dorkos/shared/relay-schemas` and the `'relay_flow'` `GENERIC_EVENTS` entry to exist). Runs in parallel with 2.1 (server emit — different packages, no file overlap; this task's tests use a directly-invoked mock event handler, not a real server round-trip, so it does not need 2.1 to have landed).
tasks.activeForm: Adding the mesh relay-flow store and SSE bridge hook

tasks.subject: [relay-flow-animation] [P4] Render the offset-path pulse in BindingEdge (direction, LOD, reduced-motion), mount the subscription + reset() in TopologyGraph, RTL tests including reduced-motion and LOD branches
tasks.description: Render the single `--color-primary` dot on `BindingEdge` tracing the exact `edgePath` via CSS `offset-path` + `motion`, gated on activity/reduced-motion/zoom, and mount the SSE→store bridge once at the topology-graph host. VISUAL (apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/ui/BindingEdge.tsx) — the component already computes `zoom` via `useStore(zoomSelector)` (:14,53) and `edgePath` via `getBezierPath` (:56-63), and reads `id`/`data` as `EdgeProps` (:43-52). Add, inside `BindingEdgeInner`, before the returned JSX: `const activity = useRelayFlowStore((s) => s.activity[id]); const clear = useRelayFlowStore((s) => s.clear); const prefersReduced = usePrefersReducedMotion(); const showPulse = !!activity && !prefersReduced && zoom >= PULSE_MIN_ZOOM;` — importing `useRelayFlowStore` from `../model/relay-flow-store` (task 3.1), `usePrefersReducedMotion` from `../lib/use-reduced-motion` (already imported by sibling mesh components, e.g. AgentNode), and `PULSE_MIN_ZOOM` from `../config/relay-flow-constants` (task 3.1). Render, inside the existing returned fragment after the `<BaseEdge .../>` element (:79-86 region): `<AnimatePresence>{showPulse && (<motion.circle key={activity.nonce} r={3} className="fill-primary" style={{ offsetPath: \`path("${edgePath}")\` }} initial={{ offsetDistance: activity.direction === 'inbound' ? '0%' : '100%', opacity: 0 }} animate={{ offsetDistance: activity.direction === 'inbound' ? '100%' : '0%', opacity: [0, 1, 1, 0] }} exit={{ opacity: 0 }} transition={{ duration: PULSE_DURATION_MS / 1000, ease: 'easeInOut' }} onAnimationComplete={() => clear(id)} />)}</AnimatePresence>` — import `motion, AnimatePresence` from `motion/react` (the house animation lib, per `contributing/animations.md`) and `PULSE_DURATION_MS` alongside `PULSE_MIN_ZOOM`. This is a sibling of `BaseEdge` in the same SVG group, so `offset-path` positions it in the same flow coordinates `BaseEdge`'s `d` uses; React Flow's ancestor transform maps both to screen identically (pan/zoom track for free — no extra transform math). `r=3`, `fill-primary` (matches the edge's existing `stroke-primary` classes at :81-84), `duration 0.8s` (`PULSE_DURATION_MS / 1000`) ease-in-out, opacity keyframes `[0,1,1,0]` (fade in then out — a heartbeat, not a laser); no trails, no color cycling, per the spec's Calm Tech tuning. MOUNT SITE (apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/ui/TopologyGraph.tsx) — the component already computes `const relayEnabled = useRelayEnabled();` at :105. Add `useRelayFlowSubscription(relayEnabled);` (imported from `../model/use-relay-flow-subscription`, task 3.1) once at the top level of the component body (not inside the `useMemo`/`useEffect` blocks — it's a hook call, not a derived value), and add a `useEffect` that calls `useRelayFlowStore.getState().reset()` on unmount (cleanup-only effect: `useEffect(() => () => { useRelayFlowStore.getState().reset(); }, []);`), so any orphaned activity entries are cleared when the topology view unmounts — matching the store's own `reset()` TSDoc contract from 3.1. This mounts the bridge exactly once for the whole graph (not per-edge), consistent with the spec's Detailed Design §5 guidance. RTL TESTS (apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/ui/__tests__/BindingEdge.test.tsx — the existing suite already mocks `@xyflow/react` with `useStore: (selector) => selector({ transform: [0, 0, 1] })` giving `zoom = 1` by default, and defines `BASE_EDGE_PROPS` including `id: 'binding-edge-1'`) — add: mock `../model/relay-flow-store` (`useRelayFlowStore`) and `../lib/use-reduced-motion` (`usePrefersReducedMotion`) the same way the existing `AgentNode.reduced-motion.test.tsx` mocks its LOD/reduced-motion hooks (`vi.mock('../../lib/use-reduced-motion', () => ({ usePrefersReducedMotion: () => mockUsePrefersReducedMotion() }))`), and add: (a) with a mocked active store entry for `id='binding-edge-1'`, `zoom=1` (≥ `PULSE_MIN_ZOOM`), reduced-motion `false` → a pulse element renders (assert via `screen.getByTestId` or a `fill-primary` class query on the rendered circle) — purpose: the render gate honors activity; (b) reduced-motion mocked `true` (same active entry, same zoom) → NO pulse element renders — purpose: Decision 5, the explicit testable reduced-motion gate; (c) `useStore` mocked to return `transform: [0, 0, 0.3]` (zoom below `PULSE_MIN_ZOOM = 0.5`) with an active entry and reduced-motion `false` → NO pulse element — purpose: the LOD gate; (d) no active store entry (idle edge) → NO pulse element, and the existing edge-path/label rendering is unaffected — purpose: idle edges stay clean. Acceptance: `BindingEdge` renders the pulse only when `activity && !prefersReduced && zoom >= PULSE_MIN_ZOOM`; the pulse traces the exact `edgePath` via `offset-path`, animates in the correct direction, fades via opacity keyframes, and calls `clear(id)` via `onAnimationComplete`; `TopologyGraph` mounts `useRelayFlowSubscription(relayEnabled)` once and resets the store on unmount; all 4 new RTL cases pass alongside the existing BindingEdge test suite. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/client lint && pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/ui/__tests__/BindingEdge.test.tsx apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/ui/__tests__/TopologyGraph.test.tsx. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 6 (the pulse visual, exact JSX and rationale for offset-path over animateMotion), §5 (mount site — 'Mounted once in the topology host... alongside reset() on unmount'), §Testing Strategy (BindingEdge RTL — all 4 branches), §Performance Considerations (by-edge-id selector — no extra re-renders on idle edges), §Code structure & file organization table. Depends on 3.1 (needs `useRelayFlowStore`, `useRelayFlowSubscription`, and the pulse constants to exist). Runs in parallel with 2.1 if 2.1 has not yet landed when 3.1 completes — this task's tests exercise the store directly via mocks, not a real server round-trip, so it does not need the server emit wired.
tasks.activeForm: Rendering the BindingEdge pulse and mounting the subscription bridge

tasks.phaseName: P5 — Dev Playground, changelog, draft ADR, follow-up pointers
tasks.subject: [relay-flow-animation] [P5] Dev Playground synthetic relay_flow showcase, changelog fragment (Added), draft ADR, and recorded pointers for the two DONE-stage follow-ups (outbound direction; agent↔agent edges)
tasks.description: Close out the feature with its honest visual-QA surface and the required documentation/decision artifacts, and leave explicit pointers for the two follow-ups the spec defers to DONE. Because a real end-to-end pulse needs a live external adapter delivering through a binding to a running agent — not reliably drivable in Playwright, and Mesh+Relay sits behind the AGENTS.md demo-claim gate — the spec explicitly recommends a Dev Playground showcase as the honest manual-verification path (it doubles as the demo surface without claiming live relay works). DEV PLAYGROUND SHOWCASE — per the `maintaining-dev-playground` skill, this is a genuinely new, stateful, animated component (multiple visual states: idle/pulsing, reduced-motion, LOD-culled) so it qualifies for a showcase. Add a new section to apps/client/src/dev/sections/topology-sections.ts's `TOPOLOGY_SECTIONS` array (which already contains `agentnode`/`adapternode`/`namespacegroupnode`/`edge-styles`/`topologylegend` entries, category `'Edges'` for the existing `edge-styles` entry): `{ id: 'relay-flow-pulse', title: 'Relay Flow Pulse', page: 'topology', category: 'Edges', keywords: ['relay', 'flow', 'pulse', 'animation', 'binding', 'edge', 'traffic', 'topology'] }`. Add the corresponding showcase component to apps/client/src/dev/showcases/TopologyShowcases.tsx (or a new co-located file if the existing file is already near the 500-line split threshold — check first) rendering a minimal `BindingEdge` (or a small isolated SVG host wrapping it, reusing the real `BindingEdge` component per the maintaining-dev-playground skill's parity rule — 'render the SAME component in both the playground and the application', never rebuild the visual) with a control (button or auto-firing interval) that calls `useRelayFlowStore.getState().pulse('binding:demo-edge', 'inbound')` directly — a synthetic trigger that bypasses the SSE stream entirely (no live relay/adapter needed), exercising the exact same store + BindingEdge render path task 4.1 built. Include a toggle or two side-by-side edges to demonstrate per-edge coalescing (rapid clicks on one edge collapse to one pulse) and cross-edge concurrency (two edges pulsing independently). CHANGELOG FRAGMENT — create changelog/unreleased/<id>-relay-flow-animation.md where <id> is a timestamp id from `.claude/scripts/id.ts` (filename pattern `<YYMMDD-HHMMSS>-relay-flow-animation.md` per changelog/README.md; no frontmatter; Keep-a-Changelog headings). This is user-visible (a new live visual on the Agents map) so it is an **Added** entry in `writing-for-humans` voice. Use the spec's own draft copy as the basis (§Documentation): "### Added\n- The Agents map now shows live traffic: when a message reaches one of your agents from a connected app, a dot travels the wire between them. It respects your reduced-motion setting. (DOR-167)" — rewrite in your own plain words if needed, keep it honest and demo-gate-safe (describe what the user sees; do not claim Mesh+Relay is verified end-to-end). Never edit CHANGELOG.md directly (ADR 260707-231641). DRAFT ADR — prefer running `/adr:from-spec` against `specs/relay-flow-animation/02-specification.md` (applies the significance rubric at extraction, writes `decisions/<id>-<slug>.md` plus the `decisions/manifest.json` entry using a timestamp id from `.claude/scripts/id.ts`); otherwise write it by hand per the `writing-adrs` skill. Status: proposed. Title: "Relay flow animation broadcasts metadata-only relay_flow events at the inbound binding-routing boundary." Capture, per spec §Related ADRs verbatim: the privacy posture (routing skeleton only — bindingId/adapterId/agentId/direction/at, never payload, text, subject, or chatId); the dedicated-event-vs-relay_message choice (the console feed carries content and stays untouched); the delivered-only honesty gate (deliveredTo > 0, riding the DOR-260 budget + DOR-277 consent gates); the clean server-side join (BindingRouter holds binding.id/adapterId/agentId, avoiding the sessionId↔agentId + DOR-335 identity hazards); and the inbound-only-v1 scope with outbound deferred for lack of a binding-aware outbound site. Cross-link DOR-260, DOR-277, and ADR-0310 (the `/api/events` fan-out this event joins). Add the `decisions/manifest.json` entry with status `proposed`. FOLLOW-UP POINTERS (recorded here, NOT filed as tracker issues — DECOMPOSE does not write to the tracker; filing them is DONE stage's job per the `flow__closing-work` skill, which creates follow-up work as part of closing DOR-167) — write a clearly labeled 'Follow-ups for DONE' note into this task's own completion summary (e.g. the PR description, or a short section at the bottom of the draft ADR's Consequences) naming exactly two items so DONE has concrete pointers rather than having to re-derive them from the spec: (1) **Outbound (agent→adapter reply) pulses** — deferred because the agent reply publishes to a `relay.inbox.*` reply inbox (packages/relay/src/adapters/claude-code/publish.ts:44-52,141; agent-handler.ts:203,316), not back through `BindingRouter` (`handleInbound` explicitly skips `from.startsWith('agent:')`, binding-router.ts:244) — no single server-side site currently resolves an outbound reply to `{bindingId, adapterId, agentId}` without a reverse-binding lookup; the schema's `direction` field already accepts `'outbound'` so it drops in cleanly once that lookup exists. (2) **Agent↔agent flow** — no such topology edge renders today (only `binding:{id}` adapter↔agent edges exist, per build-topology-elements.ts:199-213); surfacing it needs a new edge type plus a new `relay.agent.*` broadcast, its own scoped work. Acceptance: the Dev Playground showcase exists, is registered in `TOPOLOGY_SECTIONS`, reuses the real `BindingEdge` component (not a rebuilt visual), and demonstrates coalescing + cross-edge concurrency via synthetic store writes with no live relay/adapter dependency; the changelog fragment exists with a valid timestamp-id filename and an Added heading in plain, honest, demo-gate-safe language; `decisions/<id>-<slug>.md` exists with status `proposed` capturing the five items above plus the DOR-260/DOR-277/ADR-0310 cross-links; `decisions/manifest.json` is valid JSON with the new proposed entry; the two follow-ups are recorded in a findable, explicitly labeled spot for DONE to pick up. Verify: node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('decisions/manifest.json','utf8'))" (confirms it parses) && pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/client lint && a manual load of `/dev` → Topology page → 'Relay Flow Pulse' section, confirming the synthetic pulse animates and respects reduced-motion (no automated visual-regression test — this is the honest manual-QA path per the spec) plus a manual writing-for-humans self-check on the changelog copy. Cites: spec §Testing Strategy ('E2E — not driven, stated honestly' — the Dev Playground showcase recommendation), §Documentation (the changelog fragment's exact draft copy, the draft-ADR pointer, OpenAPI already regenerated in 1.1), §Related ADRs (the Proposed ADR's full content list), §Non-Goals (outbound deferral reasoning, agent↔agent follow-up reasoning), §References (the exact file:line citations for both follow-ups). Depends on 2.1 (the ADR documents the server emit's exact honesty gate and join keys, which must be landed code, not just spec prose, to cite accurately) and 4.1 (the Dev Playground showcase renders the real `BindingEdge` pulse component, and the changelog describes the shipped visual — both need the visual to exist, matching the repo's own precedent of sequencing user-facing prose after the surface it describes). No further parallelization — this is the closing task; nothing else remains once 2.1 and 4.1 have landed.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the Dev Playground showcase, changelog fragment, draft ADR, and follow-up pointers
